rulings.Case Title: TEODORO HOLGADO VS. JOSHUA GALAMAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2022, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT TEODORO HOLGADO's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. OFF CALENDAR, Dismissals of Complaint and Cross-Complaint on file. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD PATTERSON VS. SANDRA SAUCEDO-ALVAREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2022, line 3. DEFENDANT SANDRA SAUCEDO-ALVAREZ's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Hearing required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on February 9, 2022 Defendant to file and serve evidence of her presence in Bolivia and establishing residency there: airplane tickets; copy of passport showing entry and exit from and to Bolivia; buying property; setting up utilities; registration to vote; doctor's appointments; bank accounts in local banks; ATM withdrawals; gym memberships, etc. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SF BAKER INVESTMENTS LLC VS. STEPHEN SNODGRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2022, line 8. DEFENDANT STEPHEN SNODGRASS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. DENIED. Defendant provides no evidence that the Summons was issued in error based on the information available to the Court Clerk on December 17, 2021 (Motion 3:3) and provides no admissible competent evidence indicating that Plaintiff's verification on UD-101 was false. Defendant further provides no authority for the proposition that wrong form of summons was issued on December 17, 2021 or that service of such summons is insufficient to confer jurisdiction. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
